blocks.content: They found a low concrete barrier near one of the quieter rides — not a bench, just a ledge, the way two people who have been friends long enough can make any surface do.
The carnival continued around them. Here, in this corner, it was muted — the noise a background thing rather than a foreground one.

Lena didn't open with the subject. She looked at the afternoon for a moment, said something about the light on the Ferris wheel doing something interesting. Then she went quiet in the particular way that meant she was making room.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I've been trying to figure out how to say it for months. And I still don't—

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: What are you most afraid of? Right now. Today.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: That it changes how he holds things. The way he carries it. That knowing will be — a weight for him that I put there, that I can't take back.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: He's already chosen. I know he has. I could feel it even before he said anything. And I — I want him to have made that choice with the full picture. Because he deserves that. And because without it—

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Without it, I feel like I'm letting him love something that isn't entirely real.
